content.full_text:
• 
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Michael R. Nave 
Meyers, Nave, Riback & West 
1220 Howard Avenue, suite 250 
Burlingame, CA 
94010-4211' 
Dear Mr. Nave: 
June 8, 1988 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. G-88-211 
You have written for advice concerning application of 
Article XII, Section 7, of the California Constitution to Bay 
Area Rapid Transit District directors . 
. 
The Fair Political Commission is responsible for 
interpretin~ and enforcing the provisions of the Political 
Reform Act.!! 
Your concerns are not addressed by the Act. 
Therefore, we are unable to provide advice on this subject . 
We appreciate your interest in the Commission. 
If we may 
be of assistance in the future, please let me know. 
DMG:plh 
Sincerely, 
Q ~ 
~ ;;:L ira 
Diane M. Griffiths l ... 
General Counsel 
11 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 9r:;H04~nR()7 • 
{Q I hI l'7 '7 3;'h.h.{~ 


:NIEYERS, :-;[A VE, RIBACK & WEST 
May 31, 1988 
(41 
FAX(415)342-0886 
i41 
892-8878 
REPLY 
Peninsula Office 
Legal Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Re: 
Request for Advice 
California Constitution, Article XII, Section 7 
Gentlepersons: 
Article XII, Section 7 of the California Constitution 
provides as follows: 
"A transportation company may not grant free 
passes or discounts to anyone holding an 
office in this state; and the acceptance of 
a pass or discount by a public officer, 
other than a Public Utilities Commissioner, 
shall work a forfeiture of that office. 
A 
Public Utilities Commissioner may not hold 
an official relation to nor have a financial 
interest in a person or corporation subject 
to regulation by the commission." 
In view of the foregoing prohibition, mayan elected 
Director of the Bay Area Rapid Transit District (BARTD) accept 
and/or use a pass or other privilege for free transportation on 
a bus or train owned and/or operated by BARTD without forfeiting 
the directorship of BARTD? 
Your reply will be appreciated. 
Very t 
rs, 
MEYERS, NAVE, 
&

sections.question: In view of the foregoing prohibition, may an elected Director of the Bay Area Rapid Transit District (BARTD) accept and/or use a pass or other privilege for free transportation on a bus or train owned and/or operated by BARTD without forfeiting the directorship of BARTD?
sections.conclusion: Your concerns are not addressed by the Act. Therefore, we are unable to provide advice on this subject.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether an elected BART Director can accept and use free transportation passes without forfeiting their office under California Constitution Article XII, Section 7.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the question concerning California Constitution Article XII, Section 7 is not addressed by the Political Reform Act, which is the statute the FPPC is responsible for interpreting and enforcing.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter. The incoming request from the attorney is included in full before the FPPC's response. Document dated June 8, 1988 with file number G-88-211. FPPC response is brief and provides no substantive analysis.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: In view of the foregoing prohibition, may an elected Director of the Bay Area Rapid Transit District (BARTD) accept and/or use a pass or other privilege for free transportation on a bus or train owned and/or operated by BARTD without forfeiting the directorship of BARTD?

CONCLUSION: Your concerns are not addressed by the Act. Therefore, we are unable to provide advice on this subject.

embedding.summary: The FPPC received an inquiry regarding whether a BART Director could accept free transportation passes without forfeiting their position under California Constitution Article XII, Section 7. The FPPC declined to provide advice, stating the matter falls outside the scope of the Political Reform Act.
